title: CTET Eligibility Criteria 2026: Educational Qualification

detail.title: CTET Eligibility Criteria 2026: Educational Qualification
detail.short_info: CTET Eligibility Criteria 2026 is released as per NCTE guidelines. Check detailed educational qualifications for Paper 1 & Paper 2, age limit, nationality, reservation benefits, and number of attempts for the CTET February 2026 exam.

content.text: CTET Eligibility Criteria 2026 is officially defined as per NCTE guidelines and released in the Information Bulletin for CTET February 2026. Candidates must fulfill the prescribed educational qualifications for Paper 1 (Classes 1-5) or Paper 2 (Classes 6-8) to appear in the exam on 08 February 2026.

The minimum educational qualifications for CTET are prescribed by the National Council for Teacher Education (NCTE). Candidates must ensure they meet the required qualifications before applying. The eligibility differs for Paper 1 and Paper 2. Relaxation of up to 5% in qualifying marks is provided to reserved category candidates. It is important to note that appearing in CTET does not guarantee eligibility for appointment; the final verification is done by the recruiting agency.

sections.heading: CTET Eligibility Criteria for Paper 1 (Classes 1 to 5) – Primary Stage

content.text: For teaching Classes 1 to 5, candidates must have passed or be appearing in the final year of the following qualifications:

items: Senior Secondary (or equivalent) with minimum 50% marks and 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education (D.El.Ed), OR
Senior Secondary with 45% marks and 2-year Diploma in Education (Special Education), OR
Senior Secondary with 50% marks and 4-year Bachelor of Elementary Education (B.El.Ed), OR
Graduation with 50% marks and 2-year D.El.Ed, OR
Any other qualification prescribed by NCTE.

content.text: This ensures candidates have both subject knowledge and pedagogical skills suitable for primary stage teaching.

sections.heading: CTET Eligibility Criteria for Paper 2 (Classes 6 to 8) – Elementary Stage

content.text: For teaching Classes 6 to 8, candidates must have passed or be appearing in the final year of:

items: Graduation with 50% marks and 2-year D.El.Ed, OR
Graduation with 50% marks and 1-year B.Ed, OR
Graduation with 45% marks and 1-year B.Ed (Special Education), OR
Senior Secondary with 50% marks and 4-year B.El.Ed, OR
Senior Secondary with 50% marks and 4-year BA/B.Sc.Ed or BA.Ed/B.Sc.Ed, OR
Any other NCTE prescribed qualification.

content.text: Candidates aiming to teach both levels should apply for both papers.

There is no upper age limit for appearing in the CTET examination. Candidates only need to fulfill the minimum age criteria (if any) prescribed by the respective state governments or recruiting bodies for teacher appointments. This makes the exam accessible to fresh graduates as well as experienced candidates looking to improve their qualifications or switch to teaching careers. The absence of an upper age bar encourages lifelong learning and career opportunities in the education sector. However, candidates should check the recruitment notification of the specific school/organization they wish to join, as some may have their own age criteria.

Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply for the Central Teacher Eligibility Test. There is no provision for candidates from other nationalities. This criterion aligns with government recruitment rules for teaching positions in central government schools (KVS, NVS, etc.) and other institutions where CTET is applicable. Candidates must provide valid proof of Indian nationality if required during document verification at the time of appointment.

sections.heading: CTET Eligibility Criteria for Reserved Category

content.text: Candidates belonging to SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), and Differently Abled categories receive a 5% relaxation in the minimum qualifying marks in educational qualifications as per NCTE guidelines. For example, where general category candidates need 50% marks, reserved category candidates need only 45%. The application fee is also lower for reserved categories — ₹500 for one paper and ₹600 for both papers (plus GST). Differently abled candidates can avail scribe facilities, compensatory time of 50 minutes per paper, and large-print question papers as per RPwD Act 2016. Proper disability certificates are required to claim these benefits.

sections.heading: CTET 2026 Exam Eligibility: Number of Attempts

content.text: There is no restriction on the number of attempts for CTET. Candidates can appear as many times as they want to improve their scores. The CTET qualifying certificate is now valid for a lifetime for all categories. This policy allows candidates to keep attempting until they achieve a good score and also gives them the flexibility to appear again even after qualifying once. Qualifying CTET is one of the essential eligibility criteria for teacher appointments but does not guarantee a job.
Step-by-step process:

items: Visit the official website https://ctet.nic.in
Click on “Apply Online”
Fill the authentication form and register
Complete the online application form
Upload scanned photograph and signature
Pay the examination fee online
Take a printout of the confirmation page

content.text: Candidates must keep their Class 10th details ready and upload latest photographs as per specifications. [Apply Online Link]
